prestazioni: Engine swap to a naturally aspirated 3.2L Type 911/50 (930/20) flat-six from a 1987 Porsche 911 Carrera, retaining the original 914/6 transaxle (Type 901/03) with a custom bellhousing adapter by G50 Solutions for compatibility. Upgraded to a custom 3.5-inch stainless steel exhaust system by Fabspeed Motorsport with 4.5-inch Euro-style tips and a 2.5-inch center section, paired with a Borla Pro XS muffler for aggressive yet street-legal sound levels.

Full suspension overhaul using Ohlins Road & Track DFV coilovers (914-specific valving) with adjustable camber plates by Elephant Racing, paired with Tarett Engineering spherical bearing front control arms and rear trailing arm reinforcements. Polyurethane bushings by Pelican Parts for all suspension mounts, and a custom 22mm front sway bar by 914World with adjustable end links.

Forced induction via a Rotrex C30-94 supercharger kit (914-specific) by TPC Racing, running 8 psi of boost with a custom air-to-water intercooler setup by APR Performance. Fueling upgrades include Bosch 044 high-flow fuel pump, Siemens DEKA 80lb/hr injectors, and a custom ECU tune by Motec M150 with individual cylinder trims for the 3.2L flat-six. Ignition system upgraded to MSD 6AL-2 with Blaster SS coils and NGK R7437-9 racing plugs.
estetica: Full widebody conversion using a 914-6 GT replica kit by Autocraft Steelcraft, featuring hand-formed steel fenders with integrated brake cooling ducts, custom aluminum rocker panels, and a rear diffuser by 914 Designs. Front bumper replaced with a carbon fiber 914/6 GT-style unit by TechArt, including integrated LED turn signals and a tow hook cover.

Custom aero package by Ruf Automobile, including a carbon fiber front splitter with adjustable dive planes, a rear ducktail spoiler (911 SC-style) with integrated third brake light, and side skirts with air curtains for brake cooling. Full carbon fiber hood by 914 Designs with a functional NACA duct for the intercooler setup.

Interior stripped and rebuilt with a full Alcantara headliner by Katzkin, Recaro SRD Evo seats (914-specific mounting brackets by Elephant Racing), and a custom roll cage by Autopower Industries with integrated harness bar. Steering wheel replaced with a Momo Prototipo (350mm) wrapped in Alcantara, paired with a quick-release hub by NRG Innovations. Gauge cluster upgraded to a digital display by AIM MXS Strada with data logging for boost, AFR, and oil temps.
